# Development Station New Chat Handoff

Date: 2026-05-18
Project: Development Station Power Supply
Phase: PCB revision focus (regulator + HAT)

## Current Objective
Continue hardware revision updates first. Firmware feature expansion remains paused until Rev1 board bring-up is complete.
Code-side work is scaffold-only until schematic population checkpoints are complete.

## Stop Point Summary
- Regulator and HAT Rev-B netlists were regenerated and reviewed after schematic edits.
- Sense-B source mapping was corrected to HAT Rev-B netlist and verified:
  - 5V via R59/R60
  - 3.3V via R61/R62
  - Adj via R63/R64
- RB-001 implementation is present on all three channels in regulator Rev-B netlist:
  - U5/U6/U7 LMV358IDR buffers
  - D4/D5/D6 BAT54C combine stage
  - R31/R33/R35 = 100 ohm local-input series
  - R32/R34/R36 = 33k local attenuation to GND
- Design intent was finalized as remote-primary sensing with slightly underbiased local fallback to reduce measurement handoff step while preserving startup fallback behavior.
- Bench validation is intentionally deferred and remains required for final closure.

## Rev1 Firmware Isolation (completed)
To prevent workflow contamination while hardware changes continue:
- Added isolated Rev1 folder: src/rev1/
- Copied active files into Rev1 scope:
  - src/rev1/main.cpp
  - src/rev1/display_main.cpp
  - src/rev1/power_telemetry_map.h
  - src/rev1/lvgl_demo.cpp
- Updated platformio.ini environments to build from src/rev1/*
- Added explicit src root excludes in platformio.ini
- Preserved copied last-rev behavior in Rev1 files for upcoming board bring-up/testing
- Preserved inversion diagnostic toggles and legacy wake path for controlled triage only

## Rules For Next Session
1. Stay on hardware revision tasks only unless explicitly redirected.
2. Do not begin bench execution steps.
3. If firmware edits are required, only edit files under src/rev1/.
4. Treat HANDOFF.md as source of truth, then this file.
5. Keep firmware in scaffold-freeze mode: no new runtime behavior changes unless a schematic-driven blocker is identified.

## Priority Order Next Session
1. Preserve current 3-channel topology unless bring-up data requires a value retune.
2. Capture every hardware delta in handoff docs before ending session.
3. Keep firmware in scaffold-freeze mode unless a schematic-driven blocker requires a narrow src/rev1 change.

## First Action Next Session
Read HANDOFF.md, then verify the latest Rev-B netlist still contains U5/U6/U7, D4/D5/D6, and R31-R36 values before making additional topology edits.

## Suggested Prompt For New Chat
Continue Development Station hardware revision work from HANDOFF.md and NEW_CHAT_HANDOFF.rmd. Keep scope on regulator/HAT Rev-B schematic and netlist updates only. Preserve the current 3-channel LMV358 plus BAT54C selector topology unless bring-up data drives value changes. Do not run bench steps unless explicitly requested. Keep firmware paused unless absolutely required, and if needed, edit only src/rev1 files.
